page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at a base clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance is configured with 8 dedicated physical cores, providing 8 vCPUs with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations position the ecs.hfc6.2xlarge in the average tier across most standard compute and memory operations. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1244.0 and a multi-core score of 4795.0. In memory bandwidth tests, it records 426.41 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, which drops to 49.31 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For database workloads, the server handles 7.45 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 2943.34 on Passmark database operations.

A notable performance tradeoff is observed in large-scale language model inference. While the server processes a small 135M model at 130.25 text generation tokens per second, performance drops significantly to a weak 6.44 tokens per second for a medium 7B model. Given the lack of local storage and the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage. It is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, lightweight database operations, and general-purpose compute tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor, it delivers 16.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data places its performance in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 4795.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 7.45 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weak performance on medium-sized LLM inference tasks. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for compute-heavy applications with moderate memory requirements, making it suitable for software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight database workloads.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average performance for web serving and compilation.

tagline: A dedicated eight-core compute-optimized instance delivering balanced processing power and moderate memory for standard web serving and compilation workloads.
